spec_match.rationale: Page exercises the concept interactively but stops short of touching the real API surface — the underlying spec behaviour is simulated rather than invoked.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive controls drive the page (14 buttons, 1 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges, 15 listeners) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the required chromestatus.com/feature/5592399713402880 link — breaks the slug self-heal.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the 'request-close' Invoker Command spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/form-elements.html#attr-button-command-request-close-state) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 3 requires every page to embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/5592399713402880. The self-heal slug-fix script reads this link to recover from misslugged folders. Add it under a 'see also' section.

summary: 'Nested Dialogs' misses a non-trivial part of the rubric (1 fail / 1 partial). The open questions list captures the highest-leverage fixes — usually adding a real interactive control or wiring the chromestatus reference link.
